agent_output: {"summary":{"verdict":"BLOCK","one_liner":"Security-critical token system uses broken MD5 hash, is vulnerable to timing attacks, and has a design flaw that makes validation impossible in practice."},"critical_findings":[{"title":"MD5 is cryptographically broken — unsuitable for security tokens","risk":"MD5 has known collision and preimage attacks. An attacker with any leaked token can forge tokens for arbitrary partner IDs. NIST deprecated MD5 for security use in 2011. For a B2B API token system, this is a direct path to unauthorized access.","severity":"Critical","location":"generatePartnerApiToken and validatePartnerApiToken — crypto.createHash('md5')","reproduction":"An attacker intercepts or obtains one token, then uses publicly available MD5 collision tools (e.g., HashClash) or rainbow tables to derive the secretKey or forge a valid token for a different partnerId.","fix":"Replace MD5 with HMAC-SHA256: `crypto.createHmac('sha256', secretKey).update(payload).digest('hex')`. This provides both cryptographic strength and proper keyed authentication. The secretKey becomes the HMAC key rather than being concatenated into a plain hash input (which is also vulnerable to length-extension attacks).","time_to_fix":"15 minutes","acceptance_criteria_affected":"Token security, partner authentication integrity"},{"title":"Token validation is impossible — timestamp from generation is never returned","risk":"generatePartnerApiToken calls Date.now() internally but only returns the hex token. validatePartnerApiToken requires the exact timestamp to reconstruct the expected token. The caller has no way to know the millisecond-precision timestamp used during generation, so validation will ALWAYS fail unless the caller somehow captures Date.now() at the exact same millisecond. This means the system is fundamentally broken — either tokens can never be validated, or the timestamp is being guessed/hardcoded elsewhere, which defeats its purpose.","severity":"Critical","location":"generatePartnerApiToken return type — returns only `string`, not `{ token, timestamp }`","reproduction":"Call generatePartnerApiToken('partner1', 'secret'). Receive a hex string. Now try to call validatePartnerApiToken with that token — you don't have the timestamp. Validation fails 100% of the time.","fix":"Return a structured object `{ token: string, timestamp: number, expiresAt: number }` so the timestamp can be stored and used for validation. Alternatively, encode the timestamp into the token itself (e.g., `base64(timestamp).hexToken`) so it can be extracted during validation.","time_to_fix":"20 minutes","acceptance_criteria_affected":"Core functionality — token validation is non-functional"},{"title":"Timing-attack vulnerable string comparison on security tokens","risk":"Using `===` for token comparison leaks information about how many leading characters match via response-time analysis. Over many requests, an attacker can reconstruct a valid token byte-by-byte. This is a well-documented attack vector against token validation endpoints.","severity":"High","location":"validatePartnerApiToken — `return token === expectedToken`","reproduction":"An attacker sends thousands of validation requests with tokens that share progressively more leading bytes with the real token, measuring response times to determine which prefix is correct.","fix":"Replace with constant-time comparison: `return crypto.timingSafeEqual(Buffer.from(token, 'hex'), Buffer.from(expectedToken, 'hex'))`. Add a length check first since timingSafeEqual throws on mismatched lengths.","time_to_fix":"5 minutes","acceptance_criteria_affected":"Token forgery resistance"}],"important_issues":[{"title":"No token expiration enforcement","description":"Tokens are described as 30-day lived, but validatePartnerApiToken never checks whether the timestamp is within a valid window. Any token, no matter how old, will pass validation. Add: `if (Date.now() - timestamp > 30 * 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000) return false;`","location":"validatePartnerApiToken — missing expiry check"},{"title":"Length-extension attack via naive concatenation","description":"Appending secretKey to the payload (`payload + secretKey`) before hashing is vulnerable to length-extension attacks with MD5/SHA-1/SHA-256. Even after switching from MD5, use HMAC (which has inner/outer padding) rather than raw hash(message + key).","location":"Both functions — `expectedPayload + secretKey`"},{"title":"No input validation on partnerId","description":"If partnerId contains a colon, the payload format `partnerId:timestamp` becomes ambiguous. A partnerId of `foo:12345` with one timestamp could collide with `foo` and a different timestamp. Validate that partnerId contains no delimiter characters, or use a structured format like JSON.","location":"generatePartnerApiToken — payload construction"}],"suggestions":["Consider using a standard token format like JWT with HMAC-SHA256 signing — it solves timestamp embedding, expiration, and validation in a battle-tested way","Add a token version prefix (e.g., `v1_<token>`) so you can rotate algorithms without invalidating all tokens simultaneously","Implement rate limiting on the validation endpoint to reduce timing attack feasibility as defense-in-depth","Store a server-side hash of issued tokens so they can be explicitly revoked before expiry"],"production_readiness":{"deployment_risks":"This code is fundamentally non-functional (validation cannot work without the timestamp) and uses a broken cryptographic primitive. Deploying this exposes all partner integrations to token forgery.","rollback_capability":"N/A — new code, no rollback concerns.","backward_compatibility":"N/A — not yet deployed.","monitoring_coverage":"No logging or metrics on validation failures, which would be needed to detect brute-force or forgery attempts.","breaking_changes":{"api_breaking":false,"db_destructive":false,"shared_library":false,"auth_security":true,"data_pipeline":false,"config_changes":false,"details":["Auth/security: cryptographically broken token generation and non-functional validation"]},"safe_to_deploy":false,"rollback_plan":"Do not deploy. Rewrite with HMAC-SHA256, structured token return, constant-time comparison, and expiration enforcement before any deployment."},"escalations":[],"confidence":{"level":97,"high_confidence_areas":["MD5 is broken for security use — this is universally accepted","Timestamp design flaw makes validation non-functional — provable by reading the code","Timing attack via === on tokens — well-documented vulnerability"],"low_confidence_areas":[],"assumptions":["Tokens are validated over a network (timing attack relevance increases)","No additional validation layer exists outside this code"]}}
